frameworks.name: model-based fMRI decoding of working memory
frameworks.one_line: estimate WM contents from visual cortex population activity via encoding models

frameworks.name: distributed redundant cortical representation
frameworks.one_line: WM information is encoded redundantly across visual and frontoparietal maps

core_question: Do working memory representations in visual cortex remain robust to distracting visual input?
key_assumptions: WM contents can be read out from visual cortex activity via encoding models
shared neural populations encode both memorandum and distractor
manipulations: presence vs absence of an intervening distracting visual task during the memory delay
key_findings: fidelity of WM representations briefly dips when memorandum and distractor are jointly encoded
distraction induces small biases in behavioral memory errors
decoding biases in early visual cortex predict behavioral biases, unlike other regions
WM information is redundantly encoded across multiple visual field maps
interpretations: distraction transiently disrupts WM representations but redundancy protects against catastrophic loss
early visual cortex representations and WM behavior are tightly intertwined
